title: pyrus × babadagensis nothosubsp. babadagensis
intro: Pyrus × babadagensis nothosubsp. babadagensis, also known as the Babylon cherry, is a nothosubspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a hybrid between Pyrus communis and Pyrus elaeagrifolia, native to the Caucasus region. The Babylon cherry has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its fruits can be consumed as a sweet and nutritious snack, offering potential health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and immune system function.
